title: Flutter Decision Ledger & Provenance Index
description: Machine-readable ledger of every significant decision (stack/library/architecture/trade-off) made for a Flutter app, produced and appended by scripts/new_adr.py. Each entry links a requirement to an ADR to the code it produced (requirement -> ADR -> files[]), so settled choices are recalled instead of re-decided. The orchestrator (skill 01) and router (skill 28) QUERY this before re-opening a choice. ADR file format is owned by skill 18_Documentation. House style: ../../../references/CONVENTIONS.md.

schemaVersion.description: Ledger format version. Bump when the entry shape changes so consumers can detect a stale schema.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last write to this ledger.

decisions.description: Every recorded decision, in ascending ADR-number order. Append-only: entries are never deleted, only superseded/deprecated via their status.

decision.description: One significant decision = one ADR. The trace fields (requirement, files, supersedes) are what distinguish this ledger from a plain ADR index.

id.description: The immutable ADR number (e.g. 7 for ADR-0007). Monotonically increasing; matches the docs/adr/NNNN-*.md file. Never reused, never reordered.

title.description: Short human title of the decision, e.g. 'Choose Supabase over custom backend'. The primary thing a recall query reads to decide 'already decided?'.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Mirrors the ADR's Status line (source of truth). 'proposed' on scaffold; flip to 'accepted' when committed. 'rejected' entries are load-bearing too — they stop a dead idea being re-proposed.

date.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the decision was recorded. Matches the ADR Date line and the STATE.md decisions-log entry.

stage.description: Pipeline stage that made the call (e.g. '11_Backend_Integration', '42_Architecture_Debate'). Null for decisions made outside a stage. See ../../../references/PIPELINE.md.

bucket.description: What kind of significant decision this is (see SKILL.md 'What counts'). Used to filter the ledger by concern.

requirement.description: The upstream driver this decision serves: a PRD story id ('STORY-12'), a UX flow, a pipeline gate, or an issue/ticket. The START of the provenance trace. Null only when genuinely unattributable.

adr.description: Repo-relative path to the ADR file, e.g. 'docs/adr/0007-choose-supabase.md'. The middle of the trace.

files.description: lib/-relative (or repo-relative) paths the decision produced or changed — the code where this decision now lives. The END of the trace. Enables 'this file exists because of which ADR?' queries.

supersedes.description: ADR number this decision replaces, or null. When set, the named entry's status must be flipped to 'superseded' and its supersededBy set to this id. Reversals are new entries, never edits.

supersededBy.description: ADR number that replaced this decision, or null. Set automatically when a later entry declares 'supersedes' this id. Keeps the link bidirectional.
